name: py-alembic-patterns
description: Alembic migration patterns for PostgreSQL. Use when creating migrations, reviewing autogenerated migrations, or handling schema changes safely.
capabilities: Generate Alembic migration files with proper upgrade and downgrade functions
Review autogenerated migrations for dangerous patterns like drop-then-add
Implement safe patterns for adding non-nullable columns to existing tables
Create concurrent index operations to avoid table locks on large tables
Handle PostgreSQL enum type creation and modification
Resolve multi-head migration conflicts in team environments
author: CJHarmath
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown content with educational code examples. All 39 static findings are false positives caused by pattern matching on example code in markdown blocks and metadata fields. No executable code, scripts, network operations, or file system access exists in this skill.
valueStatement: Database migrations can cause data loss or downtime when done incorrectly. This skill provides tested patterns for creating, reviewing, and safely applying Alembic migrations.
userTitle: Write safe Alembic migrations for PostgreSQL
limitations: Does not execute migrations or connect to databases directly
Does not generate migrations automatically from model definitions
Patterns are PostgreSQL-focused and may not apply to other databases
Does not replace human review of migration impact on production data

useCases.title: Write production-safe migrations
useCases.description: Create migrations with proper downgrade paths that handle data correctly and avoid table locks in production environments.

useCases.title: Review migrations before deployment
useCases.description: Verify migrations follow safety patterns and will not cause downtime or data loss during production deployments.

useCases.title: Audit team migration practices
useCases.description: Ensure development teams follow PostgreSQL best practices for naming, index creation, and constraint management.

promptTemplates.title: Create a new migration
promptTemplates.scenario: Need to add a table or column
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me create an Alembic migration to add a user_preferences table with id, user_id, and theme columns. Include proper upgrade and downgrade functions.

promptTemplates.title: Review autogenerated code
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this autogenerated Alembic migration for dangerous patterns. Check for data loss risks and missing downgrade logic: [paste migration]

promptTemplates.title: Handle large table changes
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to add an index on the events table which has 50 million rows. Show me how to do this without locking the table in production.

promptTemplates.title: Data migration with backfill
promptTemplates.prompt: I am adding a status column that must be backfilled based on other columns. Show me how to implement this as a data migration with batch processing.

bestPractices: Always implement downgrade functions that fully reverse the upgrade operation
Review every autogenerated migration for drop+add patterns that cause data loss
Use CONCURRENTLY for index creation on large tables to prevent production locks
antiPatterns: Relying on autogenerated migrations without manual review for column renames
Adding NOT NULL columns without backfilling existing rows first
Leaving downgrade functions empty with just pass, making rollback impossible

faq.question: Does this skill execute migrations against my database?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides patterns and examples. You run migrations yourself using Alembic commands in your project.

faq.question: What changes does Alembic autogenerate handle correctly?
faq.answer: Table creation, column addition and removal, type changes, and foreign keys work reliably. Always verify the generated output.

faq.question: How do I migrate tables with millions of rows safely?
faq.answer: Use CONCURRENTLY for indexes, batch data updates in chunks, and add nullable columns before constraints.

faq.question: Will these patterns prevent all data loss?
faq.answer: They address common pitfalls but always backup production databases before migrations and test downgrade paths.

faq.question: Can I use these patterns with MySQL or other databases?
faq.answer: Some patterns like CONCURRENTLY are PostgreSQL-specific. General structure and review practices apply broadly.

faq.question: Why should I implement downgrade functions I may never use?
faq.answer: Downgrades are essential for rollback when issues are found after deployment. The one time you need it justifies the effort.
